sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The initial state from history[0] only shows 4 bottom palette items and 4 top row items. This is the level 1 initial state, not level 2! The history includes level 1 frames too.
Let me find the first frame of level 2 in the history.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find first level 2 frame in history
for i, entry in enumerate(history):
    if entry.frame.level == 2:
        nodes = entry.frame.segmentation['nodes']
        palette = []
        for n in nodes:
            b = n['boundary']
            if b and n['pixels'] == 16:
                r, c = b[0]
                if r >= 50:
                    palette.append((c, n['color']))
        palette.sort()
        print(f"Level 2 initial bottom palette (history index {i}):")
        for c, color in palette:
            print(f"  col {c}: {color}")
        
        top = []
        for n in nodes:
            b = n['boundary']
            if b and n['pixels'] == 16:
                r, c = b[0]
                if r <= 5:
                    top.append((c, n['color']))
        top.sort()
        print("Level 2 initial top row:")
        for c, color in top:
            print(f"  col {c}: {color}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
